Cape Coral_FL.title: Cape Coral FL home tour — $385K pool home
Cape Coral_FL.script: Nobody tells you this when you're shopping for a $385K pool home in Cape Coral — but the price on the listing isn't actually your biggest expense. I've sold dozens of homes in this market and the number one thing buyers miss is the flood zone designation. A single zone difference on the same street can swing your insurance bill by $3,000 to $5,000 every single year. That's $15,000 out of your pocket over five years — on top of your mortgage. I pulled the data on three pool homes under $390K that just hit the market this week, and two of them are in preferred flood zones with significantly lower insurance costs. The third one isn't — and most buyers would never catch that. So here's my question for you: what's your budget range, and are you prioritizing low insurance costs or pool size first? Drop it in the comments and I'll personally match you with what's available right now.

Cape Coral_FL.title: Cape Coral new construction nobody knows about
Cape Coral_FL.script: There's a new construction pocket in Cape Coral that most buyers — and honestly, most agents — have completely overlooked. I'm talking move-in ready homes, 3 beds, 2 baths, starting under $369K, in a flood zone X area, which means no required flood insurance. That alone saves you $2,400 to $4,000 every single year. I pulled the permit data myself — 14 new builds in this corridor completed in the last 60 days, and only 4 are still available. The builder is currently covering $10K in closing costs, but that incentive disappears when the remaining inventory moves. I've already walked two of my buyers through these this week. Here's what I want to know — are you looking to buy in Cape Coral in the next 3 to 6 months? Drop your timeline below and I'll send you the exact addresses before they're gone.

Cape Coral_FL.title: First time buyer? Watch this before buying in Cape Coral
Cape Coral_FL.script: If you're a first-time buyer looking at Cape Coral, there's one thing almost nobody tells you before you sign — and it can add $4,000 to $6,000 to your annual costs overnight. Cape Coral has over 400 miles of canals, and depending on which side of Burnt Store Road or Del Prado you buy, your flood insurance quote can swing from $800 a year to over $4,500 — same price home, completely different zone. I pulled the FEMA flood maps this week and found three neighborhoods under $380,000 where buyers are landing X flood zone designations — meaning no mandatory flood insurance at all. I helped two first-time buyers save over $11,000 in their first year just by choosing the right street. Are you buying in the next six months or already actively searching right now — drop it below and I'll send you the specific streets I'm watching this week.

Cape Coral_FL.title: I tracked every Cape Coral sale this month 📊
Cape Coral_FL.script: I tracked every single Cape Coral sale this month — and three things jumped out that most buyers and sellers completely miss. First, homes on the northwest side near Burnt Store Road are closing 11 days faster than anywhere else in the city right now. Second, sellers who dropped price even once are leaving an average of $14,000 on the table compared to those who priced it right from day one. Third, there are still 4 builders offering closing cost credits between $10,000 and $18,000 — but two of them cut their programs at the end of this month. I've got the full breakdown sorted by zip code, price range, and days on market. Here's what I want to know — are you buying or selling in Cape Coral in the next 90 days? Drop your situation below and I'll send you exactly what the data says about your specific move.

Cape Coral_FL.title: Cape Coral waterfront under $500K — yes really
Cape Coral_FL.script: Most people think waterfront in Cape Coral starts at $700K. They're wrong — and that mistake is costing them. Right now there are direct-access canal homes in Cape Coral under $500K, and I pulled the data this week to prove it. We're talking Gulf-access neighborhoods off Surfside Boulevard and the Savona area — actual boats-out-the-backyard waterfront, not some landlocked pond. Three bedrooms, screened lanai, dock-ready. Under $500K. I've had buyers skip Cape Coral entirely because they assumed they couldn't afford waterfront, and every single one of them regretted it when they saw what they missed. These don't sit long — two of the listings I'm tracking went under contract in under six days. Here's what I want to know: are you looking for Gulf access or fresh water canal? Drop it in the comments and I'll send you exactly what's available right now.
